content.full_text:
State of California

A-77-291

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5931          322-5441          322-6466

October 19, 1977


Mr. Ralph Faust
Executive Secretary
Agricultural Labor Relations Board
915 Capitol Mall, Room 335
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Filing of Statements of Economic Interests of Designated
    Employees Under the Conflict of Interest Code of the ALRB

Dear Mr. Faust:

    This is in response to your letter to me dated 10 October
1977. After discussing the referenced matter with the full
staff, we have come to the following conclusion which we
believe will resolve your agency's (and others similarly
situated) problem.

    Initially, let me point out one apparent misunderstanding
in your letter. Since the ALRB's Code becomes effective on
October 27, 1977, statements filed by designated employees not
later than November 26, 1977, should reflect reportable
investments and interests in real property held by those
employees as of the effective date of the Code (October 27),
not as of the exact date of filing. If you check the terms
of your Code you'll see that this is the case. Accordingly,
the "period covered" by any subsequent statement, whenever
required to be filed, will be the period from October 27,
1977, until the closing date of the statement in question.

    With this caveat, we next address your main concern.
The Commission has previously adopted a regulation dealing
with a problem analogous to the one you present as it affects
persons required to file Statements of Economic Interests
pursuant to Government Code §87200. That regulation provides,
in relevant part:

Ralph Faust
October 19, 1977                                                Page Two


     "If a person assumes an office specified in
Government Code §87200 between October 1 and
December 31 and files a Statement of Economic
Interests pursuant to Government Code §87202,
that person need not file a Statement of Eco-
nomic Interests pursuant to §87203 until one
year later than the date specified in subsec-
tion (b)."

                                        --2 Cal. Adm. Code §18723(c)


     By its express terms the regulation only applies to
persons filing pursuant to §87200, and not those filing
pursuant to a Conflict of Interest Code adopted pursuant
to §87300, et seq. As currently applied, the majority of
the staff does not feel the regulation can be interpreted
to excuse an annual filing by designated employees in the
manner suggested by your letter. However, since it was
unanimously agreed by the staff that the spirit of the
Act and the regulation would be served by complying
with your request, we have determined to amend the
regulation in an emergency fashion at the Commission
meeting of November 1-3. The amended regulation will
specifically allow agencies to follow the course of
conduct suggested in your letter, and will be effective
in time for your agency to take advantage of its provis-
ions.

     I trust this answers your question.  Thank you for
writing. I will keep you informed of the progress of
the events referred to.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division


KWG:glb

STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                                                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

AGRICULTURAL LABOR RELATIONS BOARD
915 Capitol Mall, Room 335
Sacramento, Ca 95814
(916) 322-4612

October 10, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
Mr. Kenneth Goshorn
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California     95804

Re:  ALRB Conflict of Interest Code-1977 Annual Statement

Dear Mr. Goshorn:

     As per your telephone conversation with Nancy Destefanis of
this office, I am writing to request that the Commission
waive the requirement that ALRB employees file a 1977 Annual
Statement of Economic Interests.

     The ALRB filed its Conflict of Interest Code with the Secretary of State on September 27, 1977; it becomes effective on
October 27, 1977 and all designated employees must comply by
November 26, 1977. Since the 1977 Annual Statement would cover
only a 35 day period, i.e., the period between November 26,
1977 and December 31, 1977, I am requesting that the 1977 Annual
Statement be waived. If this is acceptable, our employees would
file their 1978 Annual Statement for the period, November 26,
1977 through December 31, 1977.

     I look forward to hearing from you.

     Sincerely,

     [signature]
     RALPH FAUST
     Executive Secretary


ND:tr
cc:  Chairman Brown
     Nancy Destefanis
